feedback.text: Greetings, i write as a concerned citizen and consumer to politetly request to consider an issue of videogame ownership as an angle for the Digital Fairness Act. In todays day and age, many videogame companies have actively worked to reduce customer ownership of videogames as a product eather trough discontinuation of service for both multiplayer only and singleplayer titles, disabling games do to requring connection to game server for ambigious reasons or fraudulous Terms of Service. This leads to videogame companies being able to sell a product to a customer and then take it away with no real repercussion, something thats hardly possible with normal physical product. Example of such case is a game Overwatch, which has been disabled and unable to be played despite selling over 50 million copies, with no way for the consumer to maintain their copy. Similar "discontinuation" happened to Call of Duty: Warzone. Similarly, payment processing companies have recently made moves to use their immense influence to limit and effectively censor what games can be sold to customers under pretense of protecting children, despite all of the games being sold legaly and trough secure means with apropariate warnings and limitations to prevent ease of access for children and people underaged for such titles. Ability of any company to effectively control the digital products to the point of being able to take it away or prevent them from being sold is fundamentally anti-consumer. To conclude and restate my point, i politetly ask to consider the protection of videogames as digital products from being destroyed,censored and generally unjustly influenced by companies in anti-consumer manner. Thank you
